section: Chapter 6: Keeping Time with the Skies

questions.question: Why do we experience day and night on Earth?
questions.correct_answer: Because the Earth rotates on its axis
questions.explanation: As the Earth rotates, different parts of the planet face the Sun and then move away from it, causing day and night.

questions.question: What is the time taken by the Earth to complete one rotation?
questions.explanation: The Earth completes one full rotation around its axis in about 24 hours, creating the day-night cycle.

questions.question: What causes the phases of the Moon?
questions.correct_answer: The changing positions of the Earth, Moon, and Sun
questions.explanation: As the Moon revolves around Earth, the portion illuminated by the Sun changes, producing different phases.

questions.question: What is the time taken by the Moon to complete one cycle of phases?
questions.explanation: The Moon takes approximately 29.5 days to go from one new moon to the next.

questions.question: What is a constellation?
questions.correct_answer: A group of stars forming a recognizable pattern in the sky
questions.explanation: Examples include Orion and Ursa Major which appear as patterns in the night sky.

questions.question: What is a sundial?
questions.correct_answer: A device that tells time using the shadow of the Sun
questions.explanation: The position of the Sun changes during the day, causing the shadow to move and indicate time.

questions.question: Why were early calendars based on the Moon?
questions.correct_answer: Because lunar phases are regular and easy to observe
questions.explanation: Ancient civilizations used the Moon’s phases to track time and create calendars.

questions.question: What is a solar eclipse?
questions.correct_answer: When the Moon comes between the Sun and Earth
questions.explanation: The Moon blocks sunlight from reaching parts of the Earth during a solar eclipse.

questions.question: What is a lunar eclipse?
questions.correct_answer: When the Earth comes between the Sun and the Moon
questions.explanation: The Earth's shadow falls on the Moon during a lunar eclipse.

questions.question: How did ancient people keep track of time before clocks?
questions.correct_answer: By observing the Sun, Moon, and stars
questions.explanation: They used natural patterns in the sky to estimate time and create calendars.

notes.title: Earth’s Rotation
notes.content: The Earth rotates on its axis once every 24 hours, causing day and night.

notes.title: Phases of the Moon
notes.content: Different portions of the Moon appear illuminated as it revolves around Earth.

notes.title: Constellations
notes.content: Constellations are patterns of stars used to identify positions in the night sky.

notes.title: Sundials
notes.content: Sundials measure time using the position of the Sun and the shadow cast by a stick or pointer.

notes.title: Eclipses
notes.content: Eclipses occur when the Sun, Earth, and Moon align in a straight line.

facts.fact: The Earth rotates from west to east.
The Moon does not produce its own light; it reflects sunlight.
A lunar month lasts about 29.5 days.
Ancient civilizations used the Sun, Moon, and stars to measure time.
